cards.back: A schema is a conceptual representation of the structure of a database, including the relationships between different entities and the types of data they can hold
In a single schema architecture, all data is stored in a single database, and the schema is the overall structure of the database
In a multi-schema architecture, multiple schemas are stored in separate databases, and each schema represents a different conceptual view of the data
Data independence refers to the ability of a database to be modified, updated, or changed without affecting the applications that use it
SQL is a standard language used to manage and manipulate data in relational databases, allowing users to perform various operations such as creating, modifying, and querying data
